note_data.title: Chemical energetics
keyPoints: **Enthalpy change** (ΔH) is the heat energy change at constant pressure and temperature
**Exothermic** reactions have negative ΔH values and release heat energy to the surroundings
**Endothermic** reactions have positive ΔH values and absorb heat energy from the surroundings
**Standard enthalpy of formation** (ΔHf°) is the enthalpy change when 1 mole of compound is formed from its elements in standard states
**Standard enthalpy of combustion** (ΔHc°) is the enthalpy change when 1 mole of substance is completely burned in oxygen under standard conditions
**Hess's Law** states that the enthalpy change for a reaction is independent of the route taken
**Bond energy** is the energy required to break 1 mole of a particular covalent bond in the gaseous state
**Calorimetry** uses q = mcΔT to calculate enthalpy changes from temperature measurements
Energy is **required to break bonds** (endothermic) and **released when bonds form** (exothermic)

definitions.definition: The heat energy change at constant pressure
The enthalpy change when 1 mole of compound is formed from its elements in their standard states under standard conditions
The enthalpy change for a reaction is independent of the route taken, provided initial and final conditions are the same
The energy required to break 1 mole of a particular covalent bond in the gaseous state

howToAnswer.instruction: Give the definition or fact in one sentence with no explanation
Show all working clearly. Include units in final answer. Use appropriate number of significant figures
Give reason using 'because' or 'this is because'. Link cause and effect clearly
For energy level diagrams, label axes, reactants, products, and ΔH with arrow

modelAnswer.question: Calculate the enthalpy of formation of methane using the following combustion data: ΔHc°[C] = -394 kJ mol⁻¹, ΔHc°[H₂] = -286 kJ mol⁻¹, ΔHc°[CH₄] = -890 kJ mol⁻¹
modelAnswer.answer:
C(s) + 2H₂(g) → CH₄(g)
Using Hess's Law:
ΔHf°[CH₄] = ΔHc°[C] + 2ΔHc°[H₂] - ΔHc°[CH₄]
ΔHf°[CH₄] = (-394) + 2(-286) - (-890)
ΔHf°[CH₄] = -394 - 572 + 890 = -76 kJ mol⁻¹
markBreakdown: Mark 1: Correct equation for formation of methane
Mark 4: Correct final answer with units

commonMistakes: Never confuse bond breaking (endothermic) with bond forming (exothermic)
Students often forget to change sign when reversing reactions in Hess cycles
Always include units (kJ mol⁻¹) in final answers - marks lost if omitted
Never say 'bonds are made' - always say 'bonds are formed'
Students mix up ΔHf° and ΔHc° - formation is FROM elements, combustion is burning in oxygen
examinerTips: Hess's Law calculations appear in every Paper 2 - practice drawing cycles
Energy level diagrams must have labeled axes (Energy on y-axis, Reaction pathway on x-axis)
Bond energy questions always specify 'gaseous state' - mention this for full marks
In calorimetry, show q = mcΔT calculation even if calculator used
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Energy level diagram showing reactants and products connected by arrow labeled ΔH, with energy on y-axis and reaction pathway on x-axis. Activation energy shown as peak between reactants and products.
